{"key_event":"UN trusteeship builds institutions and electoral politics","reasoning":"Italian Somaliland’s trusteeship substantially expanded bureaucracy, courts, police, budgets, elected institutions, and Somali staffing, though British Somaliland remained lightly administered and clan authority still limited unified state reach. Civil society strengthened through parties, unions, newspapers, elections, and Pan-Somali mobilization, but one-party SYL dominance and clan-party competition kept it from becoming strongly constraining.","society_change":0.9,"society_power":5.7,"state_change":0.7,"state_power":6.0}
